description: Epic 7 — Advanced MCP Tools: Add query_code_pattern, query_dependencies, and validate_approach tools plus optional vector search provider for semantic matching

userStories.title: query_code_pattern graph module — IR search and scoring
userStories.description: As an AI agent, I want a graph-layer function that searches deserialized IR (functions, types, exports) by name matching with scored results, so that query_code_pattern has its core logic.
acceptanceCriteria: New file crates/seshat-graph/src/code_pattern.rs with pub fn query_code_pattern()
Function signature: pub fn query_code_pattern(conn: &Arc<Mutex<Connection>>, branch_id: &str, query: &str) -> Result<CodePatternData, GraphError>
Deserializes files_ir for the branch, searches function names, type names, and export names against query tokens
Scoring: exact match (1.0) > prefix match (0.7) > contains (0.4), results sorted by score descending
Each result includes: name, kind (function/type/export), file_path, line, end_line, is_public, snippet, score
Snippet truncation: max 10 lines, truncated: true flag when exceeding
Also searches conventions via existing search_conventions() for related conventions
Response structure: data.patterns[] (from IR) + data.related_conventions[] (from FTS5)
metadata includes: query, pattern_count, convention_count, search_type (keyword for V1), next_steps
Module exported from crates/seshat-graph/src/lib.rs
Unit tests: exact name match, prefix match, substring match, type search, convention results included, empty query error, no results returns empty arrays
cargo test -p seshat-graph passes
cargo clippy --all-targets -- -D warnings passes
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: Core graph-layer module. MCP handler wired in US-003.

userStories.title: query_code_pattern MCP tool handler
userStories.description: As an AI agent, I want to call query_code_pattern via MCP so that I can search for existing code patterns before writing new code.
acceptanceCriteria: New file crates/seshat-mcp/src/tools/query_code_pattern.rs with MCP tool handler
Request struct: query: String (required), kind: Option<String> (filter: function/type/export/all), scope: Option<String>, file_path: Option<String>, repo: Option<String>
Tool registered in server.rs with #[tool(description = ...)] and wired through execute_tool pipeline
impl_tool_request! macro invoked for the request type
Empty query returns ErrorEnvelope with EMPTY_TOPIC error code
No results returns empty arrays (not an error)
Call logger summary: {pattern_count, convention_count}
Integration test: register tool, call via MCP, verify response envelope structure
cargo test --workspace passes
cargo clippy --all-targets -- -D warnings passes
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: query_dependencies graph module — dependency index and blast radius
userStories.description: As an AI agent, I want a graph-layer function that builds a dependency index from IR and returns direct dependents/dependencies with blast radius for any file.
acceptanceCriteria: New file crates/seshat-graph/src/dependencies.rs with pub fn query_dependencies()
Function signature: pub fn query_dependencies(conn: &Arc<Mutex<Connection>>, branch_id: &str, target_path: &str) -> Result<DependencyData, GraphError>
Builds forward map (file_path → Vec<Import>) and reverse map (exported_module_or_name → file_path) from IR
Module resolution: relative imports resolved against importing file directory, absolute imports matched by suffix, external imports excluded
Returns dependencies[] (files target imports from) with file_path, import_names, resolved flag
Returns dependents[] (files that import from target) with file_path, import_names, line
blast_radius classification: low (<3 dependents), medium (3-10), high (>10)
blast_radius_count: exact number of direct dependents
backward_compatibility_note present when dependents exist
external_dependencies[] from dependencies_used for target file
Target path not found in IR returns GraphError (mapped to NODE_NOT_FOUND error code at MCP layer)
Module exported from crates/seshat-graph/src/lib.rs
Unit tests: file with known imports → correct dependencies, file imported by others → correct dependents, blast radius calculation, unresolved imports flagged, file not in IR → error
cargo test -p seshat-graph passes
cargo clippy --all-targets -- -D warnings passes
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: Independent of US-001/US-002. Graph-layer only, MCP handler in US-004.

userStories.title: query_dependencies MCP tool handler
userStories.description: As an AI agent, I want to call query_dependencies via MCP so that I can understand the blast radius of my changes.
acceptanceCriteria: New file crates/seshat-mcp/src/tools/query_dependencies.rs with MCP tool handler
Request struct: path: String (required), scope: Option<String>, file_path: Option<String>, repo: Option<String>
Tool registered in server.rs with #[tool(description = ...)] and wired through execute_tool pipeline
impl_tool_request! macro invoked for the request type
Target path not found → ErrorEnvelope with NODE_NOT_FOUND code (add to ErrorCode enum if needed)
Call logger summary: {dependent_count, dependency_count, blast_radius}
metadata.next_steps: Review dependents before changing public API, Call validate_approach to check for convention violations
Integration test: register tool, call via MCP, verify response envelope structure
cargo test --workspace passes
cargo clippy --all-targets -- -D warnings passes
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: validate_approach graph module — graduated response and evidence gating
userStories.description: As an AI agent, I want a graph-layer function that validates a proposed approach against rules, contradictions, duplicates, conventions, decisions, and observations with a graduated response and evidence gating.
acceptanceCriteria: New file crates/seshat-graph/src/validate_approach.rs with pub fn validate_approach()
Function signature: pub fn validate_approach(conn: &Arc<Mutex<Connection>>, branch_id: &str, params: ValidateApproachParams) -> Result<ValidateApproachData, GraphError>
ValidateApproachParams: description: String, file_context: Option<String>, approach_type: Option<String>

Graduated response sections in order: rules[] (weight=Rule conventions), contradictions[] (Contradicts edges), duplicates[] (from query_code_pattern IR search, score > 0.6), conventions[] (FTS5 match), decisions[] (user decisions), observations[] (low-confidence)
Each rules entry includes: description, evidence snippet, severity must_fix
Each duplicates entry includes: name, file_path, line, snippet, used_by count from dependency index
Verdict logic: rules_violated if any rules → warnings_found if contradictions or high-weight conventions → info_only otherwise → approved if nothing matches
Evidence gating: ready: false if verdict is rules_violated OR matched conventions have confidence < 0.5
what_would_help: Vec<String> with actionable suggestions when ready = false
summary: deterministic template counting each section
Reuses query_code_pattern graph function internally for duplicate detection (not duplicated logic)
Can optionally use query_dependencies to enrich used_by counts when file_context provided
Module exported from crates/seshat-graph/src/lib.rs

Unit tests: approach matching rule → verdict rules_violated and ready=false, approach with duplicates → duplicates populated, clean approach → approved and ready=true, evidence gating with stale conventions, what_would_help populated
cargo test -p seshat-graph passes
cargo clippy --all-targets -- -D warnings passes
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: Depends on US-001 (query_code_pattern) and US-003 (query_dependencies) graph modules.

userStories.title: validate_approach MCP tool handler
userStories.description: As an AI agent, I want to call validate_approach via MCP so that I can validate my proposed approach before writing code.

Request struct: description: String (required), file_context: Option<String>, approach_type: Option<String>, scope: Option<String>, file_path: Option<String>, repo: Option<String>
Tool registered in server.rs with #[tool(description = ...)] and wired through execute_tool pipeline
impl_tool_request! macro invoked for the request type
Empty description → ErrorEnvelope with EMPTY_TOPIC error code
Call logger summary: {verdict, rule_count, duplicate_count, convention_count, ready}
metadata.next_steps: contextual based on verdict — if rules violated: Fix rule violations; if duplicates: Consider reusing; if approved: Proceed
Integration test: register tool, call via MCP, verify full response structure
cargo test --workspace passes
cargo clippy --all-targets -- -D warnings passes
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: seshat-embedding crate — trait, Ollama and OpenAI providers
userStories.description: As a developer, I want an embedding provider abstraction with Ollama and OpenAI implementations so that vector search can be optionally enabled.
acceptanceCriteria: New crate crates/seshat-embedding/ with Cargo.toml and lib.rs added to workspace
EmbeddingProvider trait: async fn embed(&self, texts: &[String]) -> Result<Vec<Vec<f32>>, EmbeddingError> and fn dimension(&self) -> usize
OllamaProvider implementation: POST to http://localhost:11434/api/embeddings with configurable model name (default: all-minilm)
OpenAIProvider implementation: POST to https://api.openai.com/v1/embeddings with API key from env var OPENAI_API_KEY, configurable model (default: text-embedding-3-small)
Provider selection via [embedding] config section in seshat.toml: provider, model, optional dimension
Config validation: unknown provider name → clear error at startup
When [embedding] not configured: vector search disabled, zero overhead
Provider errors (timeout, API error): return EmbeddingError with enough context for graceful degradation
Unit tests: mock provider returns expected embeddings, config parsing for both providers, unknown provider error
cargo test -p seshat-embedding passes
cargo clippy --all-targets -- -D warnings passes
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: Independent of US-001 through US-006. New crate.

userStories.title: Code embeddings migration, storage, and scan integration
userStories.description: As a developer, I want embeddings generated during seshat scan and stored in SQLite so that vector search can be used by query_code_pattern.
acceptanceCriteria: New migration V6__code_embeddings.sql: code_embeddings table with id, branch_id, file_path, item_name, item_kind, embedding (BLOB), UNIQUE constraint on (branch_id, file_path, item_name, item_kind)
Migration runs successfully and is registered in the migration system
Embedding generation during seshat scan: for each function/type/export, embed {kind} {name} in {file_path} using configured provider
Batch calls to provider with configurable batch_size (default 32)
When embedding provider not configured: scan skips embedding generation entirely (zero overhead)
Embeddings stored as raw f32 bytes in BLOB column
seshat.example.toml updated with commented [embedding] section
Unit tests: embedding storage and retrieval, batch processing
cargo test --workspace passes
cargo clippy --all-targets -- -D warnings passes
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: Vector search integration into query_code_pattern
userStories.description: As an AI agent, I want query_code_pattern to use vector similarity search alongside FTS5 when an embedding provider is configured, for semantic code matching.
acceptanceCriteria: Cosine similarity function in Rust: f32 dot product, no SQLite extension needed
Vector search: load embeddings for branch, compute cosine with query embedding, return top-K results
query_code_pattern enhanced: when embedding provider configured, search_type becomes semantic
Merges FTS5 keyword results with vector results by score
metadata.search_type: keyword (FTS5 only) or semantic (FTS5 + vector)
Provider errors degrade gracefully to FTS5-only search, log warning, never crash
Unit tests: cosine similarity calculation, vector search ranking, graceful degradation on provider error, merged result ordering
Integration test: query_code_pattern with mock embedding provider returns semantic results
cargo test --workspace passes
cargo clippy --all-targets -- -D warnings passes
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: Depends on US-001 (query_code_pattern graph module) and US-008 (code embeddings storage).

userStories.title: Tool registration, cross-tool references, and next_steps updates
userStories.description: As a developer, I need all three new tools properly registered with comprehensive descriptions and existing tools updated with cross-references to the new tools.
acceptanceCriteria: All 3 new tools registered in server.rs with comprehensive #[tool(description = ...)] descriptions for AI agent discovery
Tool descriptions include: purpose, key parameters, example use cases, what to call next
validate_approach internally reuses query_code_pattern graph function for duplicate detection (verified, not duplicated logic)
validate_approach optionally uses query_dependencies to enrich used_by counts when file_context provided (verified)
Existing tool metadata.next_steps updated: query_project_context → mentions validate_approach, query_convention → mentions validate_approach, record_decision → mentions validate_approach
ErrorCode enum verified: existing codes cover all new tool error cases, NODE_NOT_FOUND added if not already present
All existing tests still pass (no regressions from next_steps changes)
cargo test --workspace passes
cargo clippy --all-targets -- -D warnings passes
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: Final integration story. Depends on all prior stories (US-001 through US-009).
